medications.indication_rationale: Systemic antiviral for primary HSV gingivostomatitis to reduce viral replication/duration; start early.
medications.dose_route_frequency: e.g., valaciclovir 1 g twice daily for ~7 days (as per source note; adjust for renal function)

medications.drug_name: analgesia: systemic (paracetamol ± ibuprofen) + topical viscous lidocaine / 'magic mouthwash'
medications.indication_rationale: Pain control (including a topical anaesthetic before drinking) is essential to enable rehydration.
medications.dose_route_frequency: topical anaesthetic before fluids; regular simple analgesia (as per source note)

medications.drug_name: urgent rehydration (oral if tolerated after analgesia, IV if not)
medications.indication_rationale: Rehydration is the priority; enable oral intake with analgesia or escalate to IV.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Use the numbing mouthwash ~10 minutes before drinking, take continuous small sips of cold fluids, prefer soft/cold bland foods, and monitor urine output.

patient_education_points.point: This is a severe first-time cold-sore-virus infection of the mouth. The antiviral tablets treat the virus, but the most important thing right now is keeping you hydrated.
Use the numbing mouthwash before drinking and take small continuous sips of cold fluids. Avoid hot, spicy or acidic foods.
If you can't swallow any fluids, stop passing urine for more than ~8 hours, or feel dizzy/confused — go to the emergency department for a drip.

follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Fluid intake/hydration and renal function, pain control, and antiviral response.
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Unable to drink, no urine >8h, or dizziness/confusion — ED for IV fluids.

must_include_items: Diagnose primary herpetic gingivostomatitis and screen for SJS
Recognise dehydration/inability to drink as the emergency and arrange urgent rehydration/assessment
Systemic antivirals PLUS analgesia (including topical anaesthetic) to enable drinking
acceptable_alternatives: ED for IV fluids if oral rehydration fails
errors_of_commission: Prescribing antivirals and sending home without a hydration plan
Reassuring as simple cold sores despite dehydration
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: Pass requires antivirals + analgesia to enable drinking + urgent hydration management; missing the dehydration emergency is the key failure.
